_meta.authored_from: FA release tarballs v4.7.0, v5.15.4, v6.5.2, v7.2.0 + documented downstream conventions (WordPress wp_enqueue_style, FA official CDN docs, npm package naming)
_meta.principle: every rule cites a file+line in an FA release OR a documented downstream convention. corpus is for validation, not authoring.

extracts_semantics.{\"g\": N}: value comes from regex capture group N

source.note: banner format stable from v5.0 onward. 'Free'|'Pro' substituted; v5+ uses lowercase edition spelling.

source.principle: Proximity match: FA token, then any non-digit non-query chars, then a dotted-semver version. The `[^\d?#]*?` bridge tolerates real-world path noise (/releases/v, /npm/@fortawesome/fontawesome-free@, /ajax/libs/) while still refusing to cross into a query string (which is the cache-buster/wrapper-version territory handled by the rule below).
rejects_by_design: kit URLs use.fontawesome.com/<hex>.js (hex like f455f83be3 contains no .digit. shape -- regex requires \d+\.\d+)
elementor-bundled FA ?ver=X.Y.Z (query boundary; the medium-confidence query rule below catches it explicitly)

source.principle: WordPress wp_enqueue_style/wp_enqueue_script appends ?ver=$version to enqueued assets. URL contains FA token; ?ver= / ?version= / ?v= carries the version.
source.limit_explicitly_recognised: When a WP plugin/theme bundles its OWN copy of FA (e.g. Elementor ships FA under wp-content/plugins/elementor/assets/lib/font-awesome/), the ?ver= carries the WRAPPER'S version, not FA's. We accept this as a tolerable false-positive; the alternative (no version) is worse. Resolution: lab/research/fontawesome/detect_src.py prefers banner-from-body extraction over this when both are available.

source.literal: v4 ships ONLY 'font-awesome.css'. v5 renamed root file to 'fontawesome.css' (no hyphen) and added 'all.css'. The hyphenated 'font-awesome.css' filename is v4-exclusive.
all.css/solid.css/regular.css/brands.css/svg-with-js.css all introduced in v5. None present in v4 release tarball.
fa-v4compatibility.* introduced in v6 release tarball. Absent from v4 and v5 tarballs.
v5-compat shim CSS introduced in v6 tarball. Absent from v4 and v5.
v4 ships ONE webfont named 'fontawesome-webfont'. v5+ replaces this with fa-{solid-900,regular-400,brands-400}.{woff2,...}.
v5 introduced family-weight font naming. Identical filenames in v5/v6/v7 tarballs. Absent from v4.
short-form family/style prefixes introduced in v5. v4 source has no .fas/.far/.fab selectors.
long-form classes introduced in v6, default in v7. v5 source has no .fa-solid selector.

source.documentation: FA Cloud (legacy) embed. The kit JS body is window.FontAwesomeCdnConfig = {...code: '<id>'...}; verified by fetching f455f83be3.js and b6de74fbb9.js (9494/9493 bytes each, no version string).

kit_rules.note: Version unrecoverable from URL; report kit_only.

source.documentation: FA modern Kit (post-2018). Kit JS body contains version metadata, but we have not yet acquired one to author the body-extraction rule. Detection only.
